revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Ōnaobi-no-Kami]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''直毘神'''（なおびのかみ、なほびのかみ）は[[神道]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において、[[黄泉]]から帰った[[イザナギ]]が[[禊]]を行って黄泉の[[穢れ]]を祓ったときに、その穢れから[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。この禍津日神がもたらす禍を直すために生まれたのが直毘神である。[[古事記]]では八十禍津日神・大禍津日神が成った後に'''神直毘神'''（かみなほびのかみ）、'''大直毘神'''（おほなほびのかみ）と[[伊豆能売]]の三柱が成ったとしている。[[日本書紀]]第五段第六の一書では八十枉津日神が成った後に'''神直日神'''（かみなほひのかみ）'''大直日神'''（おほなほひのかみ）の二柱の神が成ったとしている。同段第十の一書では少し異なっており、イザナギが禊の際に大直日神を生み、その後に大綾津日神（大禍津日神と同一神格）を生んだとしている。
ナホは禍を直すという意味である。ビは神霊を意味するクシビのビとも、「直ぶ」の名詞形「直び」であるともいう。いづれにしても、直毘神は凶事を吉事に直す神ということである。ナホ（直）はマガ（禍、曲）と対になる言葉であり、[[折口信夫]]はナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は[[巫女]]であるとも考えられる。
==関連項目== *[[日本の神の一覧]]
[[Category:日本の神|なおひ]]

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|240px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（古事記に基づく） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''直毘神'''（なおびのかみ、なほびのかみ）は[[神道]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において、[[黄泉]]から帰った[[イザナギ]]が[[禊]]を行って黄泉の[[穢れ]]を祓ったときに、その穢れから[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。この禍津日神がもたらす禍を直すために生まれたのが直毘神である。[[古事記]]では八十禍津日神・大禍津日神が成った後に'''神直毘神'''（かみなほびのかみ）、'''大直毘神'''（おほなほびのかみ）と[[伊豆能売]]の三柱が成ったとしている。[[日本書紀]]第五段第六の一書では八十枉津日神が成った後に'''神直日神'''（かみなほひのかみ）'''大直日神'''（おほなほひのかみ）の二柱の神が成ったとしている。同段第十の一書では少し異なっており、イザナギが禊の際に大直日神を生み、その後に大綾津日神（大禍津日神と同一神格）を生んだとしている。
ナホは禍を直すという意味である。ビは神霊を意味するクシビのビとも、「直ぶ」の名詞形「直び」であるともいう。いづれにしても、直毘神は凶事を吉事に直す神ということである。ナホ（直）はマガ（禍、曲）と対になる言葉であり、[[折口信夫]]はナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は[[巫女]]であるとも考えられる。
==関連項目== *[[日本の神の一覧]]
[[Category:日本の神|なおひ]]

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|240px|イザナキの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（古事記に基づく） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''直毘神'''（なおびのかみ、なほびのかみ）は[[神道]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において、[[黄泉]]から帰った[[イザナギ]]が[[禊]]を行って黄泉の[[穢れ]]を祓ったときに、その穢れから[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。この禍津日神がもたらす禍を直すために生まれたのが直毘神である。[[古事記]]では八十禍津日神・大禍津日神が成った後に'''神直毘神'''（かみなほびのかみ）、'''大直毘神'''（おほなほびのかみ）と[[伊豆能売]]の三柱が成ったとしている。[[日本書紀]]第五段第六の一書では八十枉津日神が成った後に'''神直日神'''（かみなほひのかみ）'''大直日神'''（おほなほひのかみ）の二柱の神が成ったとしている。同段第十の一書では少し異なっており、イザナギが禊の際に大直日神を生み、その後に大綾津日神（大禍津日神と同一神格）を生んだとしている。
ナホは禍を直すという意味である。ビは神霊を意味するクシビのビとも、「直ぶ」の名詞形「直び」であるともいう。いづれにしても、直毘神は凶事を吉事に直す神ということである。ナホ（直）はマガ（禍、曲）と対になる言葉であり、[[折口信夫]]はナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は[[巫女]]であるとも考えられる。

[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において、[[黄泉]]から帰った[[イザナギ]]が[[禊]]を行って黄泉の穢れを祓ったときに、その穢れから[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。この禍津日神がもたらす禍を直すために生まれたのが直毘神である。『[[古事記]]』では八十禍津日神・大禍津日神が成った後に'''神直毘神'''（かみなほびのかみ）、'''大直毘神'''（おほなほびのかみ）と[[伊豆能売]]の三柱が成ったとしている。『[[日本書紀]]』第五段第六の一書では八十枉津日神が成った後に'''神直日神'''（かみなほひのかみ）'''大直日神'''（おほなほひのかみ）の二柱の神が成ったとしている。同段第十の一書では少し異なっており、イザナギが禊の際に大直日神を生み、その後に大綾津日神（大禍津日神と同一神格）を生んだとしている。
ナホは禍を直すという意味である。ビは神霊を意味するクシビのビとも、「直ぶ」の名詞形「直び」であるともいう。いずれにしても、直毘神は凶事を吉事に直す神ということである。ナホ（直）はマガ（禍、曲）と対になる言葉であり、[[折口信夫]]はナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は[[巫女]]であるとも考えられる。

ナホは禍を直すという意味である。ビは神霊を意味するクシビのビとも、「直ぶ」の名詞形「直び」であるともいう。いずれにしても、直毘神は凶事を吉事に直す神ということである。ナホ（直）はマガ（禍、曲）と対になる言葉であり、[[折口信夫]]はナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は[[巫女]]であるとも考えられる。

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}} [[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''直毘神'''（なおびのかみ、なほびのかみ）は[[神道]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
[[穢れ]]を払い、禍（まが）を直す神とする説がある。<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html 直毘霊・原文]</ref>
[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において、[[黄泉]]から帰った[[イザナギ]]が[[禊]]を行って黄泉の穢れを祓ったときに、その穢れから[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。この禍津日神がもたらす禍を直すために生まれたのが直毘神である。『[[古事記]]』では八十禍津日神・大禍津日神が成った後に'''神直毘神'''（かみなほびのかみ）、'''大直毘神'''（おほなほびのかみ）と[[伊豆能売]]の三柱が成ったとしている。『[[日本書紀]]』第五段第六の一書では八十枉津日神が成った後に'''神直日神'''（かみなほひのかみ）'''大直日神'''（おほなほひのかみ）の二柱の神が成ったとしている。同段第十の一書では少し異なっており、イザナギが禊の際に大直日神を生み、その後に大綾津日神（大禍津日神と同一神格）を生んだとしている。

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}.
Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|ja|穢れ}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi|ja|禍}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>
== Mythological Background == In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} narrative of the {{ill|kuniumi|lt=Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamunaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).
== See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} * {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} * {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden}} * {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Unreferenced|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}.
Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|ja|穢れ}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi|ja|禍}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>
== Mythological Background == In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} narrative of the {{ill|kuniumi|lt=Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamunaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).
== See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} * {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} * {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden}} * {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

== Mythological Background == In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} narrative of the {{ill|kuniumi|lt=Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamunaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

== Mythological Background == In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} narrative of the {{ill|kuniumi|lt=Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamunaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamunaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

{{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanagi's purification (based on the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}) [[media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} deity.
Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify {{ill|Kegare|ja|穢れ}} (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>
In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, during the {{ill|Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izanome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami). == See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} * {{ill|Izanome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神}} * {{ill|Hachigami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden}} * {{ill|Mitsugo|ja|三貴子}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}}

In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami). == See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}}

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|ja|穢れ|12=draft}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=draft}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>
== Mythological Background == In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}} narrative of the {{ill|kuniumi|lt=Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|12=draft}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉|12=draft}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamunaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=draft}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=draft}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami). == See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}}
<!--Ōnaobi no Kami-->
<!--Ōnaobi no Kami--> <!--Ōnaobi no Kami-->
{{Munakata Faith}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanagi's purification (based on the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}) [[media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} deity.
Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify {{ill|Kegare|ja|穢れ|12=draft}} (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>
In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉|12=draft}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=draft}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=draft}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=draft}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izanome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=draft}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=draft}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=draft}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami). == See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachigami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsugo|ja|三貴子|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}}

{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}.
Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|ja|穢れ|12=draft}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=draft}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>
== Mythological Background == In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}} narrative of the {{ill|kuniumi|lt=Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|12=draft}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉|12=draft}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamunaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=draft}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=draft}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami). == See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}}
<!--Ōnaobi no Kami-->
<!--Ōnaobi no Kami--> <!--Ōnaobi no Kami-->
{{Munakata Faith}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanagi's purification (based on the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}) [[media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} deity.
Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify {{ill|Kegare|ja|穢れ|12=draft}} (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>
In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉|12=draft}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=draft}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=draft}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=draft}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izanome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=draft}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=draft}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=draft}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami). == See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachigami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsugo|ja|三貴子|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}}

In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=draft}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami). == See Also == * {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=draft}} * {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachigami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsugo|ja|三貴子|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}}
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [[media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''直毘神'''（なおびのかみ、なほびのかみ）は[[神道]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
[[穢れ]]を払い、禍（まが）を直す神とする説がある。<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html 直毘霊・原文]</ref>
[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において、[[黄泉]]から帰った[[イザナギ]]が[[禊]]を行って黄泉の穢れを祓ったときに、その穢れから[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。この禍津日神がもたらす禍を直すために生まれたのが直毘神である。『[[古事記]]』では八十禍津日神・大禍津日神が成った後に'''神直毘神'''（かみなほびのかみ）、'''大直毘神'''（おほなほびのかみ）と[[伊豆能売]]の三柱が成ったとしている。『[[日本書紀]]』第五段第六の一書では八十枉津日神が成った後に[[神直日神]]（かみなほひのかみ）'''大直日神'''（おほなほひのかみ）の二柱の神が成ったとしている。同段第十の一書では少し異なっており、イザナギが禊の際に大直日神を生み、その後に大綾津日神（大禍津日神と同一神格）を生んだとしている。
==関連項目== *[[禊]] *[[伊豆能売]] *[[禍津日神]] *[[祓戸大神]] *[[住吉三神]] *[[綿津見神]] *[[八神殿]] *[[三貴子]] *[[日本の神の一覧]]
==脚注・出典== {{Reflist}}

[[穢れ]]を払い、禍（まが）を直す神とする説がある。<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html 直毘霊・原文]</ref>
[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において、[[黄泉]]から帰った[[イザナギ]]が[[禊]]を行って黄泉の穢れを祓ったときに、その穢れから[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。この禍津日神がもたらす禍を直すために生まれたのが直毘神である。『[[古事記]]』では八十禍津日神・大禍津日神が成った後に'''神直毘神'''（かみなほびのかみ）、'''大直毘神'''（おほなほびのかみ）と[[伊豆能売]]の三柱が成ったとしている。『[[日本書紀]]』第五段第六の一書では八十枉津日神が成った後に[[神直日神]]（かみなほひのかみ）'''大直日神'''（おほなほひのかみ）の二柱の神が成ったとしている。同段第十の一書では少し異なっており、イザナギが禊の際に大直日神を生み、その後に大綾津日神（大禍津日神と同一神格）を生んだとしている。

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:直毘神]][[zh:直毘神]]
{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581794]]
Wikidata name: Naobinokami
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).
== See Also ==
* [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]

* {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}
* [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
* {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}
* {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugami-den|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}
* {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Noble Children|qq=}}
* [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]]

[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanagi's purification (based on the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]) ]]
'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|qq=|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|qq=|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|qq=|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).
== See Also ==
* [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]
* {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|qq=|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}

To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

Wikidata name: Naobinokami
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanagi's purification (based on the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]) ]]
'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581794]]
Wikidata name: Naobinokami
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ([[:en:Kegare|Kegare]]) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] narrative of the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], after [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], he performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify [[:en:Kegare|Kegare]] (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]] and performed a [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581794]]
Wikidata name: Naobinokami
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}.

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}} narrative of the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).
== See Also ==

* {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}

* {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugami-den|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}
* {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Noble Children|qq=}}
* {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}}
