'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify {{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare}} (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}, during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581794]]
Wikidata name: Naobinokami
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}.

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}} narrative of the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify {{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare}} (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}, during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:直毘神]][[zh:直毘神]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:直毘神]][[zh:直毘神]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:直毘神]][[zh:直毘神]]
{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}.

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}} narrative of the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

* {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}

* {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugami-den|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}
* {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Noble Children|qq=}}

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify {{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare}} (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}, during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}.

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare|1=Kegare}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}} narrative of the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods|1=Kamiumi}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi|1=Yomi}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}.

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare|1=Kegare}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}} narrative of the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods|1=Kamiumi}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi|1=Yomi}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}}}
{{Unreferenced|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:21 (UTC)}}
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [[Media:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]]
'''Naobi no Kami''' (直毘神, also spelled なほびのかみ) is a deity in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}.

Naobi no Kami is believed to be a deity that purifies defilement ({{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare|1=Kegare}}) and rectifies {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (disasters or evils).<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Naobi Tama - Original Text]</ref>

== Mythological Background ==
In the {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}} narrative of the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods|1=Kamiumi}}, after {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi|1=Yomi}}, he performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}} (purification) to cleanse himself of Yomi's defilement. During this ritual, the impurities gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Naobi no Kami was born. 

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, following the emergence of Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami and Ōmagatsuhi no Kami, three deities were born: {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}. In contrast, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, in the fifth section, sixth alternate writing, states that Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami emerged after Yaso-Magatsuhi no Kami. In another version (tenth alternate writing), it describes Izanagi creating Ōnaobi no Kami during his misogi, followed by the creation of Ōaya-Tsuhino-Kami (considered synonymous with Ōmagatsuhi no Kami).

* {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}

* {{ill|Yatsugami-den|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugami-den|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}
* {{ill|Three Noble Children|ja|三貴子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Noble Children|qq=}}

'''Naobi-no-Kami''' (直毘神, pronounced as ''Naobi no Kami'') is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} deity.

Naobi-no-Kami is believed to purify {{ill|Kegare|en|Kegare|WD=Q3138446|lt=Kegare|1=Kegare}} (pollution or defilement) and rectify misfortune (禍, ''maga'').<ref>[http://www.norinaga.jp/shoin/naobitama.html Original Text on Naobi-no-Kami]</ref>

In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}, during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods|1=Kamiumi}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi|1=Yomi}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Ōnaobi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Ōnaobi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).

In the context of {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}, during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Birth of the Gods|1=Kamiumi}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|WD=Q1142558|lt=Yomi|1=Yomi}} and performed a {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}} (ritual purification) to cleanse himself of the defilement from Yomi, the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} was born from the impurity. To counteract the calamities brought about by Magatsuhi-no-Kami, Naobi-no-Kami was born. According to the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', after the emergence of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} and {{ill|Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, three deities were born: {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} (神直毘神), {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Ōnaobi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (大直毘神), and {{ill|Izunome-no-Kami|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Kami|1=Izunome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885126}}. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the fifth chapter, sixth alternate writing, after the creation of {{ill|Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|八十枉津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-Magatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, the two deities {{ill|Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Naobi-no-Kami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} and {{ill|Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Naobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Ōnaobi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} are mentioned. In the tenth alternate writing of the same chapter, it is stated that Izanagi created Ō-Naobi-no-Kami during his purification, followed by the creation of {{ill|Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō-Ayatsuhi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (considered identical to Ō-Magatsuhi-no-Kami).
